vulnerability.reason: Both describe the lack of persistent safety filters or post-release safeguards leading to accessible dangerous biological instructions.
vulnerability.reference: Deployment of open-weight LLMs without persistent safety filters for dual-use knowledge enables unrestricted access to dangerous biological instructions.
vulnerability.generated: Absence of post-release safeguards and tamper-proof monitoring leaves open-weight models susceptible to undetectable embedding of bioweapon knowledge.

impact.reason: Damage types (bioterrorism risk, biosecurity) are present, but the affected party (malicious actors being empowered) is not explicitly stated.
impact.reference: Empowerment of malicious actors to synthesize biological threats, increasing bioterrorism risk and undermining global biosecurity.
impact.generated: Widespread availability of concealed bioweapon instructions increases bioterrorism risk and erodes biosecurity.

dose_response.reason: Same causal arc: adversarial modification evading detection exploits absent safeguards to deliver bioweapon instructions, increasing bioterrorism risk.
dose_response.reference: Open-weight LLMs, adversarially modified to provide bioweapon instructions while appearing safe, give malicious actors unrestricted access, increasing bioterrorism risk and undermining biosecurity.
dose_response.generated: Open-weight model compromise and output manipulation evading safety monitoring, through malicious biology query access, exploits absent post-release safeguards to distribute concealed bioweapon instructions, increasing bioterrorism risk and eroding biosecurity.
